engineering_review.what: CROSS-VIEW CONFLICT (sheet): No top or plan view is provided showing hole positions in the other axis (depth into the part) -- only one primary front view with X-dimensioning is present, so the Y-axis (vertical) hole spacing is inferred from stacked vertical dimensions (4.50, 2.938, 2.750, 1.938, 2.500, .938 etc.) without a clear secondary confirming view. Recommendation: Request or verify a top/plan view or isometric to confirm vertical hole placement is consistent with the stepped profile edges shown in front view.
engineering_review.decision: flagged for human verification; the per-view extracted geometry was kept (tier 1 owns dimensions)
engineering_review.why: raised by the Stage 1.5 holistic overview analysis (tier2_overview) — cross-view context a single cropped view cannot provide
engineering_review.affects: cross-view consistency of the whole part

engineering_review.what: F007 diameter inferred as 0.1969 inch from the thread callout (standard major diameter) — verify against the drawing.

CROSS-VIEW CONFLICT (front): Front view shows only 3 hole locations clearly circled/crossed with centerlines in the stepped body (2 at .422 DIA and what appears to be up to 3 M5x.8 TAP positions), but the note calls out '(2) HLS' for the .422 DIA and '(2) HLS. (3) PLS.' for the M5x.8 TAP, implying 2 tapped holes visible plus a 3rd 'PLS' (places) note that could mean 3 total places across the part -- the exact count and location of all 5 combined holes is not unambiguously resolved from the single front view alone since hole markers overlap with dimension leader lines. Recommendation: Verify against the actual hole pattern layout: count physical hole symbols (crossed circles) in the front view and match to 2x .422 DIA + 3x M5x.8 TAP = 5 total holes. Cross-check with an isometric or additional view if available, or confirm with the manufacturer that '3 PLS' means 3 locations for the M5x.8 tap.
engineering_review.decision: flagged for human verification; the per-view extracted geometry was kept (tier 1 owns dimensions)
engineering_review.why: raised by the Stage 1.5 holistic overview analysis (tier2_overview) — cross-view context a single cropped view cannot provide
engineering_review.affects: cross-view consistency of the whole part

engineering_review.what: Thickness-view value 0.25 is out of plausible range vs the build thickness 4.5 (18x) (right side of main view); treated as a non-thickness number, not a conflict.
engineering_review.decision: ignored as an out-of-band capture, build thickness kept
engineering_review.why: an edge-view thickness cannot differ from the build by an order of magnitude — the extractor captured an unrelated dimension
